feature-two-column-scene.props: {"headline":"Virtual Projects","subhead":"One workflow across repositories","bodyPrimary":"Aggregate cross-repository issues into one execution view.","leftRatio":0.4,"rightPanel":"pictogram","pictogramType":"virtual","pictogramScale":1.22}

{"headline":"Virtual Projects","subhead":"One workflow across repositories","bodyPrimary":"Mount related projects into your workspace and track all configured issues in one place.","bodySecondary":"Use shared configs for team alignment or private gitignored configs for personal dependency tracking.","leftRatio":0.4,"rightPanel":"virtual-projects-demo"}

cue.label: Introduction
cue.voice: Need to track issues from multiple repositories in one place? Virtual projects let you aggregate external project data seamlessly.

cue.label: Mount Projects
cue.voice: Mount other projects into your current workspace and query all issues together. Keep context without constant repo switching.

cue.label: Shared Config
cue.voice: Add virtual projects to a team configuration file and everyone gets the same view. Or keep a private config for personal tracking.
